context: Checkpoint 2026-05-01T02:54Z after assimilating unified Refarm host direction and validating second consumer for DS loading primitives. Branch develop remains intentionally local-ahead of origin/develop; user prefers batching local validations and pushing/watching CI only once per meaningful package. Worktree should be clean after this handoff commit. Disk remains constrained but stable at ~14GB free on /workspaces/refarm, 98% used.
current_tasks: Latest remote-pushed/CI-green baseline remains 4e02e548 `refactor(homestead): scope surface context providers` unless pushed later. Local branch should be ahead of origin/develop by 29 after this handoff commit, or ahead 28 before it.

Recent commits since the primitive-promotion checkpoint: 5988fa73 `chore(project): record unified host direction`; 9f986da8 `refactor(app-me): reuse ds loading primitives`. Earlier critical local commits remain: 9688782f `feat(ds): promote studio visual primitives`; 273712f4 `feat(homestead): share reactive element lifecycle`; 99144678 `docs(ui): record primitive promotions`; 40d60a77 `chore(project): checkpoint primitive promotions`.

5988fa73 assimilated the user's Pi analogy into the project board: `.project/decisions.json` now includes DEC-031 stating that Refarm should converge to a unified `refarm` host with TUI/Web/headless renderers rather than disconnected `apps/*`; `.pi/todos/d777f597.md` tracks planning for the unified host and renderer contract. Attempting to append a requirement through the project tool failed because older REQ-AGENT entries use schema-invalid `source: user-insight` and extra `rationale` fields; do not normalize that casually.

9f986da8 made `apps/me` a second consumer of DS loading primitives: `apps/me/src/pages/index.astro` now uses `.refarm-loading-state` and `.refarm-spinner`; app-local loading CSS was removed. `apps/me/tsconfig.json` gained `@refarm.dev/locales/*` mapping so type-check can follow Homestead source imports.

Conceptual alignment from user: Pi is a single process that can show TUI or Web depending on extension/use. Refarm should aim for a canonical host/CLI process that initializes runtime/plugins/trust/telemetry/streams/actions and can expose Web, TUI, or headless renderers. Web may involve a browser/server helper process physically, but logically it should be one host/runtime. Apps remain product/workbench distributions, not the only usage path.

Key analogy captured: Pi is agent-first (`pi-agent-core`, `pi-coding-agent`, `pi-tui`, `pi-web-ui`); Refarm is sovereign-graph/plugin-first. The future coding agent should be an actor on the Refarm host, using platform contracts instead of owning all UI/runtime itself.

Validation passed for app-me second consumer: `npm --prefix apps/me run type-check`; `npm --prefix apps/me run build`; `npm run project:validate --silent`; `git diff --check`. Prior validations still relevant: DS build, Homestead custom-element test/type-check/build, apps/dev tests/type-check/build all passed after primitive promotions.
Known Vite browser externalization warnings remain non-blocking for `node:fs/promises` and `node:path` via registry/tractor/heartwood/jco during app builds.
No cleanup was run. Disk snapshot around 14GB free, 98% used.
blockers: Do not push/watch GitHub Actions after every small slice. User preference remains: validate locally, accumulate meaningful package, then push/monitor CI once.
Disk is stable at ~14GB free but host remains 98% used. Use `npm run clean:light --silent` only if generated artifacts grow meaningfully or free space drops near 2-3GB.
CI/E2E speed work is backlogged in `.pi/todos/e1279bab.md`; do not switch lanes unless requested or after product/extensibility/UI architecture batch is ready.
Builds can rewrite `validations/sqlite-benchmark/browser/public/sqlite3-worker1.mjs` by inlining the LANG expression. Inspect and restore if it appears unless intentional.
Avoid broad Rust rebuilds unless Rust is touched. Follow AGENTS.md resource discipline if Rust becomes necessary.

Project requirements append tooling currently fails due existing schema-invalid `REQ-AGENT-*` entries with `source: user-insight` and `rationale`. Decisions append/edit is okay if kept surgical; avoid normalizing old requirement entries unless explicitly planned.
Avoid editing `packages/homestead/test/Shell.test.ts` with tools that autoformat the whole file; previous attempt produced a huge diff and was reverted.
next_actions: First command after compaction: `git status --branch --short && df -h /workspaces/refarm && git log --oneline -15`. Expect develop ahead 29 if this handoff is committed after 9f986da8, or ahead 28 before handoff commit.
Commit this handoff checkpoint as `chore(project): checkpoint unified host direction` if not already committed.

Best next architecture slice: create a concise ADR/doc, likely `docs/REFARM_HOST_MODEL.md`, defining the unified `refarm` host model: runtime responsibilities, renderer responsibilities, and what remains in apps/packages. Link it from `docs/UI_ARCHITECTURE.md` and/or `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`. Keep it conceptual; do not scaffold CLI yet unless requested.

Best next frontend/platform slice: define a minimal renderer contract vocabulary shared by Web/TUI/headless: slots, surfaces, actions, streams, status, telemetry, trust gates. This can live as documentation first; implementation can follow after second consumers are clearer.

Alternative next Homestead slice: sketch a small `@refarm.dev/homestead/sdk/host` interface for host-managed runtime/actions/renderers, but only after host model doc; do not move app-specific plugins/demos/copy.
Alternative trust slice: harden `packages/homestead/src/sdk/Shell.ts` trusted HTML rendering with a named sanitizer/trust policy before richer external surfaces; avoid broad formatting churn.
Before pushing accumulated batch: rerun scoped Homestead/app validations plus `npm run project:validate --silent`, `git diff --check`, and likely app builds; then push once and monitor CI once.
If disk drops near 2-3GB free, run `npm run clean:light --silent` and re-check `df -h /workspaces/refarm`.
open_questions: Should the unified host be `packages/cli`, `packages/refarm-host`, or both (library + bin)?
Should TUI be `packages/tui` (generic renderer primitives) plus host integration, or `packages/homestead-tui` because it understands Homestead semantics?
How much of Pi's extension surface should be mirrored vs adapted to Refarm's plugin/manifest/surface/action model?

When does the Refarm agent become good enough that the user can pragmatically stop using Pi for normal work? What minimum loop is required: sessions, file tools, shell, model routing, TUI/Web, plugin trust?
Should DS data table/list/loading primitives get visual regression/storybook stories now, or wait until more consumers exist?
Should `packages/homestead/src/sdk/Shell.ts` trusted HTML rendering get a sanitizer/trust policy before richer external external surfaces?
When pushing accumulated commits, should local checkpoint/backlog commits stay in history or be squashed/reordered?
key_decisions_pending: Next lane choice after compaction: host model ADR, renderer contract doc, second-consumer adoption, Homestead host interface, sanitizer/trust hardening, or push/CI batch.

Accepted DEC-031: Refarm should converge toward a unified `refarm` host with TUI/Web/headless renderers over shared runtime contracts; apps are distributions/workbenches, not the architecture's center.
Accepted promotion policy: apps/dev incubates; Homestead owns semantic runtime/plugin/surface mechanics; DS owns repeated visual primitives; apps keep host behavior/copy/fixtures.
Accepted UI policy remains Astro-first composition, Homestead runtime contracts, small client controllers/custom elements, no accidental React.
Boundary decision remains active: Studio is shared Homestead primitive; `apps/dev` is reference workbench. Continue enforcing this in code placement.
Whether to spend CI/E2E optimization backlog work vs continue product/extensibility/UI architecture work.
